hard.next_step: JRA 5/12: Invited Tony to Agentic AI Procurement Summit. Offering my hand in helping him create the requirements document for the vendors in the eval, as Zycus has already been notified of being shortlisted.
hard.ais_why:
• On the 3/25/2026 call, Tony Singleton was unambiguous about deprioritizing AI intake: the majority of users will bypass Merlin Intake and go straight to eProcurement, with only roughly 2% of occasional business users as Merlin candidates. He framed AI intake as useful only for the occasional non-procurement user, not for the 98% who buy from catalog.
• Tony also voiced a direct quality objection on the demo, saying the perceived AI slowness raised concerns about responsiveness, and noted that a competitor had demoed faster direct quote upload inside eProcurement. The buyer is actively benchmarking against AI alternatives and finds Zycus's flow slower today.
• The RFP itself is rated "No AI Requirement." The scope is catalog management, punch-outs to Grainger and CDW, ERP receipt flow, and rate cards: classic rules-based eProcurement. No AI POC, no AI-tied budget, no AI champion.
hard.primary_competitor: Unnamed rival ahead on demos and pricing (simpler eProcurement quote-upload)

north_star_verdict.headline: Healthy eProcurement fit but momentum has thinned: the buyer has been quiet since the 25 Mar demo, the June pricing email drew only an out-of-office auto-reply, and Tony is still in discovery across 4-5 vendors with no RFP, no budget owner, and AIS Negative.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 2026-02-23; today 2026-07-06, so ~4.5 months old and 52 days to close (2026-08-27). Stage Shortlisted since 30 Mar. Last recorded buyer conversation was the 25 Mar eProcurement demo (~3.5 months ago); the 13 Mar pricing call was not recorded. Justin emailed Tony about pricing in early June but got only an out-of-office auto-reply (4 Jun). Close date has slipped twice (27 May to 24 Jun on 4 May, 24 Jun to 27 Aug on 15 Jun). A full RFP round, down-select, commercials, MSA/SOW, legal/InfoSec, and VAI/ERP integration scoping cannot complete in 52 days; expect slip to Q4 2026 or later.
evidence: Tony Singleton, 25 Mar 2026: 'still early days, just discovery phase really at the moment' with a shortlist of 'probably 4 or 5' providers.
Tony Singleton, 25 Mar 2026: 'I don't have any timing on it right now... still in discovery phase,' will 'maybe come back with something a bit more formal in terms of initial RFI or RFP.'
SF activity: Tony's only responses since 25 Mar are out-of-office auto-replies (23 Apr, 4 Jun) to Justin's outreach, including the 4 Jun 'Pricing' thread.
SF Next_Step (JRA 5/12): Zycus offered to help Tony build the vendor requirements document, confirming the RFP has not been issued.

metrics.narrative: The pain is clear but never quantified. Tony Singleton described Americas procurement as 'very immature' with 'very manual' requisition-to-PO handling and only ~80% spend-data accuracy (discovery, 02-03 Mar 2026), and on 25 Mar reframed the core problem as directing spend to preferred agreements: 'that's the biggest gap for us today.' No ROI, savings, headcount, or cycle-time figures have been produced, and Tony admitted (23 Feb) he lacks the baseline data to build a business case for leadership. The ranked move is to co-build the baseline requisition-volume and cycle-time model with Tony so the value case can be costed.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is engaged or named. Tony Singleton is an Evaluator (SF OpportunityContactRole, PRIMARY) without budget authority who must align upward: on 25 Mar he said he had looped in a global procurement excellence director at the Swedish HQ but only to 'help support some of those discussions at a later stage' once a shortlist exists, not to fund the US start. For this $75k US-only phase the budget approver is unnamed across every field and call. The move is to get Tony to name and introduce the person who signs off the US spend before any commercial step.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are emerging from the demos rather than a formal BRD. On the 25 Mar eProcurement demo Tony probed the levers that matter to AAK: catalog management for MRO/lab supplies, supplier-network self-service catalogs, punch-outs to Grainger and CDW with AAK-account pricing, configurable multi-level approval workflows by spend threshold and category, line-level cost-center allocation across US/Canada/Mexico units, and PO/receipt push-back into the VAI ERP. He explicitly asked for a rate-card-as-catalog capability for repeat lab-testing services. Notably AI is NOT a criterion: the RFP is rated 'No AI Requirement' and Tony scoped Merlin Intake to ~2% of users. Criteria are partial because no RFP/BRD has yet been issued to formalize weighting.

decision_process.narrative: The call sequence maps the process: introductions (21 Nov 2025, 23 Feb), discovery (02-03 Mar), a broader demo where Tony brought Nigel Glover and Zycus VP Sales Mike McCarthy joined (10 Mar), a pricing discussion (13 Mar, not recorded), then a deeper eProcurement demo (25 Mar). Tony is running an informal parallel evaluation of 4-5 vendors and intends to 'identify a bit more of a short list' and then issue 'something a bit more formal, an initial RFI or RFP' with no stated timeline (25 Mar). The approval chain runs upward to the unnamed Swedish global procurement excellence director for any scale-up decision. Partial because the formal RFP gate, down-select criteria, and dates are not yet defined.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics have surfaced: no MSA/SOW, legal, procurement channel, or InfoSec discussion on any call, consistent with an early single-module $75k US-only start. The only paper signal is a future professional-services charge to migrate the ERP integration to SAP in 2-5 years, which Justin committed to confirm ('I'm gonna have to ask on that,' 25 Mar). Visibility will only come once Tony issues the RFP and names the procurement/legal channel; that is the artifact to request.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is specific, owned, and confirmed by the buyer. Tony Singleton described users finding items on the Grainger site and manually re-keying requisitions into the VAI ERP with layered email approvals, calling the flow 'horrible' (03 Mar), and flagged rogue/maverick spend ('we have no way of steering the business' to preferred suppliers) and ~80% spend-data accuracy. On 25 Mar he crystallized the compelling driver: 'the biggest focus area for us right now is more the catalog stuff... ensuring that we're directing our business to use the agreements that we want to use, and that's the biggest gap for us today.' The pain owner is Tony's lean US procurement team. It remains uncosted, which is why metrics stays partial.

champion.narrative: Tony Singleton (Procurement Manager) is a developing champion: he drives the US evaluation, is candid about his internal constraints and competitor activity, brought Nigel Glover and additional buyers into the 10 Mar demo, and wants Merlin kept in the subscription. What is still missing and now concerning: he is an evaluator without budget authority, has never opened the door to the Swedish procurement excellence director who owns scaling, and has gone quiet since 25 Mar, answering Justin's April and June outreach only with out-of-office auto-replies. The champion is cooling, so securing him means re-engaging directly, arming him with the ROI case, and getting an introduction to power.

competition.narrative: This is a live multi-vendor evaluation. Salesforce Competitors lists Coupa, GEP Worldwide, JAGGAER, and SAP Ariba, and Tony confirmed 4-5 vendors in discovery (25 Mar). The freshest competitive signal is negative for Zycus: a rival 'showed us exactly the same thing, but in the procurement solution... a lot simpler and more straightforward' for direct quote upload, and Tony flagged Zycus's Merlin flow as 'a bit slow' (25 Mar); a separate rival was ahead on demos and pricing as of 23 Feb. SAP Ariba is a latent strategic threat given AAK's planned SAP migration in 2-5 years. Zycus's wedge is the Teams-embedded guided buying for low change-management overhead plus a modular start (eProcurement now, VAI integration now, clean SAP path later). Partial because the leading rival remains unnamed.

deal_movement.summary: Deal has slowed since the 25 Mar demo: close date slipped twice, the amount was trimmed, and buyer replies since April have been out-of-office auto-replies. No new buyer-facing milestone has landed in ~3.5 months.

competitive_position.summary: Live 4-5 vendor discovery with no vendor selected as of 25 Mar 2026. The strongest CURRENT threat is the unnamed rival that demoed a simpler, faster direct quote-upload inside its procurement tool and was ahead on demos/pricing as of 23 Feb; SAP Ariba is a latent strategic threat given AAK's planned SAP migration; GEP Worldwide, Coupa, and JAGGAER are shortlist names carried from Salesforce. Zycus's wedge is Teams-embedded guided buying and a modular integrate-now, migrate-to-SAP-later path.

best_practice_check.summary: Three win-strategy levers for a cooling, single-threaded, AI-resistant discovery deal.
flags: Re-open the relationship now: Tony has answered only with auto-replies since April, so the owner must re-engage directly before AAK writes an RFP without Zycus's fingerprints.

Reframe Merlin as an included add-on, not the headline: AIS is Negative, Tony scoped intake to ~2% of users, and a rival looked faster on quote upload, so win on eProcurement catalog/punch-out fit and Teams adoption.
Map power before the down-select: the deal is single-threaded on Tony (evaluator, no budget); get an introduction to the Swedish global procurement excellence director who owns scaling.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: The Merlin/agentic story is landing poorly on this deal, but that reflects genuine buyer resistance rather than weak positioning: Ritika demoed the Merlin quote-upload flow and Justin invited Tony to the Agentic AI Procurement Summit, yet Tony still scoped intake to ~2% of users and flagged the flow as slow.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS is 2.0 Negative and the calls confirm it: Tony deprioritized Merlin Intake to ~2% of occasional users, rated the RFP 'No AI Requirement,' and praised a rival's simpler eProcurement quote-upload over Zycus's slower Merlin flow (25 Mar). AI is a liability, not a wedge, on this deal.

scope_change.detail: Tony deprioritized Merlin Intake on 25 Mar ('that's probably 2% of our user base'), rated the RFP 'No AI Requirement,' and the amount was trimmed from $80k to $75k on 10 Mar, signaling a defensive, cost-conscious, catalog-first scope.

customer_preference.evidence: Positive on eProcurement fit but no decisive lean; praised a rival's simpler quote-upload and flagged Zycus's Merlin flow as slow (Tony, 25 Mar).

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain only (manual re-keying, rogue spend, ~80% spend-data accuracy); no ROI, savings, or cycle-time numbers, and Tony still lacks baseline data (23 Feb / 03 Mar).

commentary.win_position: Win 38 is carried by champion strength +0.30 (weight 15); competitive strength +0.20 (weight 15). Offsetting it: scope narrowed vs prior — Tony deprioritized Merlin Intake on 25 Mar ('that's probably 2% of our user base'), rated the RFP 'No AI….
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 52 (roughly flat): commercial topics entering (from call evidence). Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 32 is driven by Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found; Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 14 is anchored on the stage win (38), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 52, and dragged by risk 32.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 5 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, competitive, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
